page: The UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-3xL40S is a GPU-accelerated virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 20 physical cores and 20 vCPUs at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 12.8 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it integrates three NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a total of 144 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance type, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring 4190.38 and Geekbench single-core reaching 3088.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 17663.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 62711.03, placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also robust, measuring 306.94 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 144.32 GB/sec for write operations. Text processing and compression tasks perform exceptionally well, with gzip compression reaching 38.86 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers a high ratio of GPU memory and system RAM relative to its 20 vCPUs. However, because the CPU allocation is shared, workloads requiring sustained, non-burstable 100% CPU utilization across all cores may experience variability compared to dedicated instances. Additionally, the lack of bundled local storage requires users to provision external block storage, which should be factored into the overall deployment architecture and cost-efficiency calculations.

This configuration is highly suited for GPU-accelerated workloads such as deep learning, machine learning model training, and parallel data processing. The combination of 144 GB of total VRAM and 256 GB of system memory also makes it appropriate for rendering, large-scale image processing, and complex simulations that benefit from the Ada Lovelace GPU architecture.
description: The UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-3xL40S is a high-performance, GPU-accelerated virtual server configured with 20 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor and 256.0 GB of system RAM. It features three NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a combined 144 GB of VRAM. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, as evidenced by its Geekbench single-core score of 3088.0 and multi-core score of 17663.0. While it lacks local storage, its high memory-to-core ratio of 12.8 GB and substantial GPU resources make it highly efficient for memory-intensive and parallel computing tasks. This server is optimized for workloads such as machine learning training, data science, 3D rendering, and complex simulations.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-3xL40S is a KVM-based server featuring 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 144 GB VRAM, optimized for parallel computing and machine learning.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-3xL40S is a KVM server with 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 144 GB VRAM.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring three NVIDIA L40S GPUs and 256 GB RAM for demanding parallel computing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Three NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs providing 144 GB of total VRAM
20 physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor
256 GB of system memory with 12.8 GB per core
Top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench
Best suited for machine learning, rendering, and parallel data processing
